benchmark: The three synaptic-shipped data files not previously benchmarked - coverage completed
why: An audit of synaptic's tests/benchmark/data/ found 15 files, of which 12 had been run. fiqa.json, multilongdoc_ko.json and enterprise_scenario.json had never been measured. They are now - synaptic re-run in the same pass through its own drivers, scored by its own metrics.py.

fiqa.consequence: the old default 1.5 LOSES this set; it binds the winning band from above (wins at p<=1.3) and drove the default re-derivation to 1.2 - see idf_pow_ablation.json

enterprise_scenario.what_it_is: A 12-document, 15-query agent-memory scenario (knowledge sources + graph links + 4 agent sessions), generated by synaptic's generate_data.py and consumed by its conftest with links AND session activity - not a plain IR set.

docs_only.note: synaptic wins MRR by 0.0056 = one query of 15 at rank 2 vs 1 (q13); omnifuse edges nDCG; recall tied

full_scenario_docs_links_sessions.how: synaptic = exactly its conftest: ActivityTracker sessions, reinforce on accessed, phase-2.5 weakening. OmniFuse = links as triples + Feedback.remember(session description, knowledge_accessed).
full_scenario_docs_links_sessions.note: q13 ('재고 서비스 배포 시 주의할 점', designed to require learned failure patterns) flips to OmniFuse 1.000 vs synaptic 0.333 - the memory axis works as the scenario intends. OmniFuse wins nDCG and recall; synaptic keeps MRR because dense-toy links noise omnifuse's fusion on q08/q11/q12.

enterprise_scenario.the_q13_diagnosis: Per-term scoring: the docs-only loss is a token-length artifact, not IDF - one matched 3-char word (서비스 -> 3 tokens x 2.72 = 8.16) outvoted two matched relevant words. idf_pow is not the cause (p=1.0 loses q13 identically).
enterprise_scenario.honest_verdict: On this 12-doc toy OmniFuse wins nDCG in every config, wins recall, wins the designed memory query under full data, and loses MRR by one rank-2. No principled fix exists at n=12 - see the rejected experiment below - and a targeted one would be label-fitting. Recorded as the boundary, not adjusted away.

rejected_2026_07_10_word_mass_query_weig.idea: Lucene-SynonymQuery-style: tokens emitted from one word share one unit of query mass (1/k each), killing the bias where a k-char Hangul stem contributes k tokens.
rejected_2026_07_10_word_mass_query_weig.principled: parameter-free, uniform across languages, query-side only (index unchanged)
rejected_2026_07_10_word_mass_query_weig.result: Pareto catastrophe: 11 of 12 suite sets got worse and three flipped to losses (Allganize-ko 0.9535, Ko-StrategyQA 0.6372, MIRACL-ko 0.9376), finreg fell 0.8400 -> 0.7965 with multi-hop 107 -> 104 - while winning back only the one enterprise query. Reverted.
rejected_2026_07_10_word_mass_query_weig.lesson: Length-proportional bigram mass is not a bug at corpus scale: a fully matched long compound (외국환거래법) is genuinely stronger evidence than one short word. The bias only misleads when the corpus is too small for IDF to mean anything (12 docs).
